subject: chemistry
title: 19M.2.HL.TZ1.7
question_html:
An aqueous solution of silver nitrate, AgNO3 (aq), can be electrolysed using platinum electrodes.

Formulate the half-equations for the reaction at each electrode during electrolysis.

Cathode (negative electrode):

Anode (positive electrode):
solution_html:
Cathode (negative electrode):
Ag+ (aq) + e− → Ag (s)    [✔]


Anode (positive electrode):
2H2O(l) → O2 (g) + 4H+ (aq) + 4e−    [✔]

 

Note: Accept 4OH− (aq) → O2 (g) + 2H2O(l) + 4e−

Accept multiple or fractional coefficients in both half-equations.
examiners_html: Very few answers were correct, even for stronger candidates. Many failed to formulate the correct half equation for the reaction at the anode and used the nitrate ion instead of oxidation of H2O. Some candidates lost one of the marks for using equilibrium arrows in an electrolysis equation.
